pattern: Only the two fully-synchronized rotations (everyone clockwise, or everyone counterclockwise) avoid a collision, so the answer is every direction combination minus those two, taken modulo the given prime.
hints: Each of the n monkeys independently picks one of two directions, so there are 2^n total direction combinations.
A collision is avoided only when every monkey rotates the same way around the polygon; any mixture of directions forces two neighbors together.
Subtract those two collision-free combinations from the total and reduce modulo 1e9 + 7, computing the power with fast modular exponentiation since n can be huge.

edge_inputs.description: n large enough that 2^n overflows a 32-bit integer if computed naively before reducing
n large enough that 2^n overflows a 64-bit integer if computed naively before reducing

stress.description: random polygon sizes across the full allowed range
